message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Track new issues: Cranelift JIT compilation track (bd-vs3rb), LIKE
function registry bug fix (bd-wa26g), and value slab completion
(bd-wi4w4).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-vr4u","title":"§12.10-12.17 Transaction Control + ATTACH + EXPLAIN + VACUUM + Expressions + Type Affinity + Time Travel","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §12.10-§12.17 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-7pxb — §12.10-12.12 Transaction Control (BEGIN/COMMIT/SAVEPOINT) + ATTACH/DETACH + EXPLAIN
- bd-d2m7 — §12.10 BEGIN CONCURRENT + Cross-Database Two-Phase Commit
- bd-1mrj — §12.13-12.14 VACUUM + Other Statements (PRAGMA, .commands)
- bd-16ov — §12.15-12.16 Expression Syntax + Type Affinity Rules
- bd-cfj0 — §12.17 Time Travel Queries (Native Mode Extension: AS OF COMMIT)

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:39.108097323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:35:14.952857972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:39:48.352457310Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Superseded by finer-grained bd-7pxb (§12.10-12.12) + bd-1mrj (§12.13-12.14) + bd-16ov (§12.15-12.16) + bd-cfj0 (§12.17)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-1x2z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-257u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-31t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2042,"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §12.10-12.17 Transaction Control + ATTACH + EXPLAIN + VACUUM + PRAGMA + Expressions + Type Affinity + Time Travel

### Transaction Control (§12.10)
BEGIN [DEFERRED|IMMEDIATE|EXCLUSIVE|CONCURRENT]; COMMIT; END (=COMMIT); ROLLBACK.
SAVEPOINT name; RELEASE [SAVEPOINT] name; ROLLBACK TO [SAVEPOINT] name.

**Transaction modes:** DEFERRED (default, no locks until first read/write). IMMEDIATE (RESERVED lock immediately). EXCLUSIVE (EXCLUSIVE lock immediately; equivalent to IMMEDIATE in WAL mode). CONCURRENT (FrankenSQLite extension: MVCC concurrent writer mode with SI, conflicts on same page → SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT).

**Savepoints:** Stack-based. RELEASE X commits work since SAVEPOINT X and pops X + all more recent. ROLLBACK TO X undoes work since SAVEPOINT X but leaves X on stack.

### ATTACH/DETACH (§12.11)
`ATTACH expr AS schema-name; DETACH schema-name`. Main = "main", temp = "temp". Max 10 attached (SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED). Cross-database transactions atomic only in rollback journal mode (standard SQLite); FrankenSQLite MUST support cross-database atomic WAL transactions via 2PC across WAL files.

### EXPLAIN (§12.12)
`EXPLAIN stmt` → VDBE bytecode (addr, opcode, p1-p5, comment).
`EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN stmt` → high-level plan (id, parent, notused, detail). Tree via id/parent.

### VACUUM (§12.13)
`VACUUM [schema]; VACUUM [schema] INTO filename`. Rebuilds database: create new, copy all, replace original. INTO = compact backup without modifying original.

### Other Statements (§12.14)
REINDEX [collation|table-or-index]. ANALYZE [schema|table-or-index] → sqlite_stat1/stat4.
PRAGMA [schema.]name [= value | (value)].

### Expression Syntax (§12.15)
Pratt parser (normative precedence in §10.2). Key: `NOT x = y` → `NOT (x = y)`. ESCAPE parsed as LIKE suffix. Unary binds tighter than COLLATE.

**Special forms:** CAST, CASE, EXISTS, IN, BETWEEN, COLLATE, LIKE/GLOB with ESCAPE, RAISE (trigger-only), JSON -> and ->> operators.

### Type Affinity Rules (§12.16)
Five affinities: TEXT, NUMERIC, INTEGER, REAL, BLOB.

**Comparison affinity rules:** (1) If one operand has INT/REAL/NUMERIC and other has TEXT/BLOB → apply numeric to TEXT/BLOB. (2) If one TEXT and other BLOB (no numeric) → apply TEXT to BLOB. (3) Same class or both BLOB → no conversion.

**Key distinction:** Affinity applied to operand needing conversion, not both. If both share class, no coercion.

### Time Travel Queries — Native Mode Extension (§12.17)
`SELECT ... FROM table FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF 'timestamp'` or `AS OF COMMITSEQ N`.

**Semantics:** (1) Determine target_commit_seq (binary search markers for timestamp, or direct for COMMITSEQ). (2) Create synthetic read-only snapshot S with S.high = target_commit_seq. (3) Execute query using normal MVCC resolution.

**Restrictions:** Time travel is read-only (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL → SQLITE_ERROR). History pruned → explicit error. Tiered storage: fetch symbols on demand under Cx budgets.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vrdo","title":"Implement CTE (WITH clause) support","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:47:36.452809421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T08:03:02.507256441Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T08:03:02.507238537Z","close_reason":"Implemented CTE (WITH clause) support via table materialization in commit 5efb72e. 4 tests passing, all quality gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vs3rb","title":"Track N: Cranelift JIT Compilation — Eliminate VDBE Interpreter Overhead for Hot Queries","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:16:17.492722792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T23:35:10.081683508Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vs3rb","depends_on_id":"bd-87bp2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:51:18.300346500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3386,"issue_id":"bd-vs3rb","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Problem (from alien-graveyard §8.3)
The VDBE interpreter loop costs 0.10-0.15 µs per row: opcode fetch → match dispatch → operand decode → execute → next. For a simple INSERT with ~10 opcodes, that's 10 match dispatches. C SQLite amortizes this differently but Umbra/DuckDB prove JIT eliminates it entirely.

## Solution: Cranelift JIT for Prepared Statements
When a statement is prepared and executed N times (threshold: 3), JIT-compile it to native code via Cranelift. The JIT-compiled version:
1. Eliminates match dispatch (opcodes become straight-line native code)
2. Eliminates register file indexing (values live in CPU registers)
3. Eliminates type checks (column types are baked into generated code)
4. Enables CPU branch prediction (no indirect jumps)

## Alien Graveyard Reference: §8.3 (HyPer/Umbra query compilation)
Cranelift compiles 10x faster than LLVM, making it viable for per-query JIT.

## Why This Is Practical
Cranelift is already a mature Rust crate (used by Wasmtime). FrankenSQLite already has a VDBE bytecode representation — translating opcodes to Cranelift IR is mechanical. Start with just INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (simple linear programs), not complex SELECT with joins.

## Expected Impact: -0.06 to -0.12 µs per row (eliminates interpreter dispatch entirely)

## Hybrid Strategy
- Cold queries (< 3 executions): interpret normally
- Hot queries (≥ 3 executions): JIT-compile and cache native code
- This avoids JIT overhead for one-shot queries while accelerating repeated DML
","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:16:17Z"},{"id":3399,"issue_id":"bd-vs3rb","author":"ubuntu","text":"## CRITICAL SAFETY NOTE: unsafe_code workspace policy
AGENTS.md states `unsafe_code = "forbid"` at workspace level. Cranelift JIT generates native code via safe Rust APIs — it does NOT require `unsafe` blocks in our code. The Cranelift crate itself uses unsafe internally (like all code generators), but our integration layer stays in safe Rust.

If we need to call JIT-generated code, use cranelift-jit's safe execution API. Do NOT add `#[allow(unsafe_code)]` to any crate for this feature. If Cranelift's API doesn't support safe execution, fall back to the interpreter — the JIT is an optimization, not a requirement.
","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:42:04Z"},{"id":3419,"issue_id":"bd-vs3rb","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Composition with Track H (Pre-computed Headers)
When both Track H and Track N are enabled:
- The JIT compiler can embed the pre-computed header bytes as immediate constants in the generated native code
- This means MakeRecord in JIT mode = single memcpy of header template + column data copy
- No runtime varint encoding at all — everything is compile-time constant
- This is the IDEAL composition: H provides the data, N provides the execution speed

## Composition with Track O (SIMD)
When both Track N and Track O are enabled:
- JIT-generated code can emit SIMD intrinsics directly (Cranelift supports AVX2 codegen)
- The SIMD record serialization becomes part of the JIT-compiled INSERT path
- No function call overhead for the SIMD path — it's inlined native code
","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:56:39Z"},{"id":3426,"issue_id":"bd-vs3rb","author":"ubuntu","text":"## SIZE CONCERN (Review Pass 5): Cranelift Adds Significant Weight

Cranelift (cranelift-codegen + cranelift-frontend + cranelift-jit) adds:
- ~2-3 MB to binary size (release, stripped)
- ~30-60 seconds to compile time
- 15+ transitive dependencies

For a project whose value proposition includes "embedded, zero-config" like SQLite, adding a JIT compiler is a SIGNIFICANT architectural decision, not just a performance optimization.

### Recommendation
Make cranelift an OPTIONAL feature flag: `[features] jit = ["cranelift-codegen", "cranelift-frontend", "cranelift-jit"]`
- Default: OFF (no JIT, no binary bloat)
- Opt-in: `cargo build --features jit` for users who want maximum perf

### Alternative: Specialized Hard-Coded Fast Paths
Instead of full JIT, hand-write optimized Rust functions for the 5 most common INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE patterns:
1. INSERT INTO t VALUES (?, ?, ?) — fixed column count
2. INSERT INTO t VALUES (?, ?, ?) WHERE INTEGER PK — with rowid
3. UPDATE t SET col=? WHERE rowid=? — point update
4. DELETE FROM t WHERE rowid=? — point delete
5. INSERT ... SELECT — bulk insert

These specialized functions bypass the VDBE interpreter entirely for these patterns.
Cost: ~500 lines of Rust. Benefit: ~80% of JIT's benefit with zero new dependencies.

### Priority: Remains P1 (speculative, high effort, opt-in only)
","created_at":"2026-03-31T23:35:10Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-wa26g","title":"BUG: Prepared statement query_row() returns instantly for LIKE/string queries on large tables — likely returning wrong results","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:41.852450268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:26.061947426Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:26.061629381Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 813b5f8f: emit_like_expr used lowercase 'like' but registry stores uppercase 'LIKE'. All 8 LIKE tests now pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3429,"issue_id":"bd-wa26g","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Bug Description
Prepared statement `query_row()` for LIKE, string function, and GROUP_CONCAT queries returns in ~150-300 nanoseconds on 100K-row tables. C SQLite takes 14-69ms for the same queries. This means FrankenSQLite is NOT actually executing the query.

## Reproduction
```rust
let conn = Connection::open(":memory:").unwrap();
conn.execute("CREATE TABLE docs (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT, body TEXT, tag TEXT)").unwrap();
// Insert 100K rows
let stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM docs WHERE title LIKE 'Document 1%'").unwrap();
let result = stmt.query_row(); // Returns in ~150ns instead of ~15ms — WRONG
```

## Expected Behavior
query_row() should scan all 100K rows, apply the LIKE filter, and return COUNT(*) matching rows. This should take ~15ms, not ~150ns.

## Likely Root Cause
The prepared statement path (`query_row()` → `query_prepared_row_after_background_status()` → `try_execute_prepared_query_row_fast_path()`) has a fast path or deferred execution path that short-circuits for these query shapes without actually executing the query.

Alternatively: the `deferred_query_statement` path (connection.rs:2365) may be falling through to a no-op execution.

## Impact
- Benchmark results for string/pattern queries are completely invalid
- Any user code using prepare() + query_row() for LIKE/string queries may get wrong results
- This is a CORRECTNESS bug, not just a performance issue

## Where to Investigate
1. connection.rs:8198 — try_execute_prepared_query_row_fast_path()
2. connection.rs:2365 — deferred_query_statement path
3. connection.rs:8173 — query_prepared_row_after_background_status() full flow
4. Check: does query_row() return QueryReturnedNoRows or an actual Row with wrong COUNT?

## Files
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (prepared statement execution)
- crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/comprehensive_bench.rs:2632-2657 (benchmark code)
","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:42Z"},{"id":3430,"issue_id":"bd-wa26g","author":"ubuntu","text":"## ROOT CAUSE FOUND: "no such function: like/2"

The LIKE operator is implemented as a function call `like(pattern, value)`. The 2-argument form is missing from the function registry in certain code paths.

### Evidence:
- 3 LIKE tests fail with: `Internal("no such function: like/2")`
- 5 LIKE tests PASS (different execution paths that handle LIKE as a special operator)
- The prepared statement path triggers the function lookup for `like/2`
- The direct execution path handles LIKE differently (as a built-in operator)

### Fix Required:
Register the `like` function (2-argument and 3-argument forms) in the function registry, OR ensure the prepared statement path handles LIKE as a special operator like the direct path does.

### Where:
- Function registry: crates/fsqlite-func/src/builtins.rs
- Prepared statement execution: crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs
- LIKE operator handling: search for `like` in the VDBE codegen/engine paths
","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:34:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-wba3f","title":"WASM 1.2: Create wasm-compatible sync primitive polyfills (Mutex, RwLock, Once, thread_id)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:15:44.594818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T21:11:15.318738Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T21:11:15.318677Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1dzd2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1xmfa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2043,"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Task 1.2: Wasm-Compatible Sync Primitive Polyfills

## What
Create a shared module providing single-threaded sync primitives for wasm32 targets. On native targets, these re-export `parking_lot` types. On wasm32, they provide lightweight single-threaded implementations.

## Why
`parking_lot` depends on OS-specific mutex primitives that don't exist in wasm32. Since browser WASM runs single-threaded within a Worker, we can use trivial single-threaded implementations (RefCell-based or no-op).

## Detailed Design

### Module Location
Create `crates/fsqlite-types/src/sync_primitives.rs` (since fsqlite-types is the foundation crate imported by all others).

### API Surface
```rust
// Re-exports that work on all platforms
pub use crate::sync_primitives::{Mutex, MutexGuard, RwLock, RwLockReadGuard, RwLockWriteGuard, Once, OnceCell};

#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
mod native {
    pub use parking_lot::{Mutex, MutexGuard, RwLock, RwLockReadGuard, RwLockWriteGuard, Once};
    // Also re-export from std where needed
}

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod wasm {
    use std::cell::{RefCell, Ref, RefMut};

    pub struct Mutex<T>(RefCell<T>);
    impl<T> Mutex<T> {
        pub fn new(val: T) -> Self { Self(RefCell::new(val)) }
        pub fn lock(&self) -> MutexGuard<'_, T> { MutexGuard(self.0.borrow_mut()) }
        pub fn try_lock(&self) -> Option<MutexGuard<'_, T>> {
            self.0.try_borrow_mut().ok().map(MutexGuard)
        }
    }
    pub struct MutexGuard<'a, T>(RefMut<'a, T>);
    // ... Deref/DerefMut impls

    pub struct RwLock<T>(RefCell<T>);
    impl<T> RwLock<T> {
        pub fn new(val: T) -> Self { Self(RefCell::new(val)) }
        pub fn read(&self) -> RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> { RwLockReadGuard(self.0.borrow()) }
        pub fn write(&self) -> RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> { RwLockWriteGuard(self.0.borrow_mut()) }
    }
    // ... etc.
}
```

### Thread ID Polyfill
```rust
#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
pub fn current_thread_id() -> u64 { std::thread::current().id().as_u64().get() }

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub fn current_thread_id() -> u64 { 0 } // Single-threaded, always thread 0
```

### Time Polyfill
```rust
#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
pub fn now_ms() -> f64 { std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_millis() as f64 }

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub fn now_ms() -> f64 { js_sys::Date::now() }
```

### Crossbeam-Epoch Polyfill
For wasm32, epoch-based reclamation is unnecessary (single-threaded = no concurrent readers). Provide a no-op implementation:
```rust
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub mod epoch {
    pub struct Guard;
    pub fn pin() -> Guard { Guard }
    // Atomic<T> wraps a simple Cell<T>
}
```

### Key Constraints
- Must be `Send + Sync` on native targets (parking_lot provides this)
- On wasm32, `Send + Sync` bounds are less strict but some trait bounds may need `#[cfg]` adjustments
- The polyfill must have identical API surface to avoid `#[cfg]` at every call site
- Performance on native must not regress (zero-cost re-export)

### Acceptance Criteria
- Module compiles on both native and wasm32 targets
- All existing code that uses parking_lot can switch to `use fsqlite_types::sync::{Mutex, RwLock}`
- Zero performance regression on native (verified by existing benchmarks)
- `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p fsqlite-types` passes
","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:16:05Z"},{"id":2044,"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTIONS (found during deep audit, 2026-03-02)

### Issue 1: Condvar polyfill missing
fsqlite-pager/src/arc_cache.rs:28 uses `parking_lot::{Condvar, Mutex}`. The polyfill must also include Condvar:
```rust
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub struct Condvar;

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
impl Condvar {
    pub fn new() -> Self { Self }
    pub fn wait<T>(&self, _guard: &mut MutexGuard<'_, T>) {
        // No-op on wasm32 (single-threaded — nothing to wait for)
        // In practice, code paths using Condvar should be gated out
    }
    pub fn notify_one(&self) { /* no-op */ }
    pub fn notify_all(&self) { /* no-op */ }
}
```

### Issue 2: fsqlite-types will need a new dependency on parking_lot
Currently fsqlite-types does NOT depend on parking_lot. Since the polyfill lives in fsqlite-types, adding:
```toml
[target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies]
parking_lot = { workspace = true }
```
This changes the dependency graph — all crates that depend on fsqlite-types will transitively get parking_lot on native. This is acceptable since most already depend on it directly.

### Issue 3: web_time or js_sys dependency for time polyfill
The time polyfill using `js_sys::Date::now()` requires js-sys as a wasm32 dependency:
```toml
[target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
js-sys = "0.3"
```
Alternative: use the `web-time` crate which provides a drop-in replacement for std::time that works on wasm32:
```toml
[target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
web-time = "1.1"
```
The `web-time` approach is simpler because it provides `Instant` and `Duration` with the same API as std::time.

### Issue 4: getrandom/js propagation
The polyfill module should document that downstream crates using `rand`, `argon2`, or any crate that transitively depends on `getrandom` MUST add `getrandom = { version = "0.2", features = ["js"] }` to their wasm feature dependencies. This is a well-known wasm32 gotcha.
","created_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-wi4w4","title":"M1: Thread-local value slab — pool SqliteValue allocations to eliminate per-row malloc/free","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:15:47.143986677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T02:45:25.584328368Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T02:45:25.583537477Z","close_reason":"Committed in 9abedc20: value slab in value.rs (+72 lines)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3385,"issue_id":"bd-wi4w4","author":"ubuntu","text":"## What
Create a thread-local slab allocator for SqliteValue. Pre-allocate 256 slots. On value creation: pop from slab (1 ns). On value destruction: push back to slab (1 ns). Only fall back to global allocator when slab is exhausted.

## Where
- crates/fsqlite-types/src/value.rs: add thread_local! { static VALUE_SLAB: RefCell<Vec<SqliteValue>> }
- In SmallText::new() and Blob creation: try slab first
- In Drop impl: return to slab instead of dropping

## This Is The Simplest Win
No architectural changes needed. Just a 50-line thread-local pool. Eliminates 90% of malloc/free calls for SqliteValue.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero malloc calls during steady-state INSERT (after warmup)
2. INSERT benchmark: ≥ 3% faster
3. No memory leak (slab drains on thread exit)
","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:15:47Z"},{"id":3417,"issue_id":"bd-wi4w4","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Risk & Fallback
**Risk**: Thread-local slab could hold memory indefinitely if thread is long-lived but idle.
**Mitigation**: Slab has a high-water mark of 256 values. If slab grows beyond 256, excess values are freed to global allocator. Idle timeout: if no alloc for 10 seconds, shrink slab to 64.
**Fallback**: If slab causes issues, disable via compile-time feature flag `value_slab`. The code path falls back to normal Arc/heap allocation.
","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:56:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-wnk1r","title":"S3-FIFO buffer pool eviction policy for PageCache","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:56:35.297391272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T04:03:14.701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
